artifacts.title: Promoting Optimal Development: Developmental Surveillance and Screening
assertions: Developmental surveillance includes eliciting parent concerns, developmental history, observation, risks and strengths, documentation and shared findings at health supervision visits.

Standardized general developmental screening is recommended at 9, 18 and 30 months, with evaluation or referral when surveillance raises concern; this package records screening provenance and does not reproduce a licensed tool.

artifacts.title: Learn the Signs. Act Early developmental monitoring
assertions: Milestones span social-emotional, language-communication, cognitive and movement domains; missed or lost skills and caregiver concern should be shared promptly.
CDC milestone checklists are monitoring aids and are not substitutes for standardized validated developmental screening.

artifacts.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management
assertions: Growth assessment uses dated weight and length or height plotted with previous measurements; BMI and parental height context may be relevant by age.
Clinical, developmental and social assessment plus detailed feeding history and, when appropriate, feeding observation support evaluation without indiscriminate testing.

artifacts.title: WHO Child Growth Standards
assertions: WHO standards provide length or height, weight, weight-for-length or height, BMI, head circumference and velocity references; the package preserves raw measurements and chart identity rather than assigning centiles itself.

artifacts.title: STOM paediatric growth and development terminology lookup
assertions: FHIR lookup confirmed active developmental-delay, failure-to-thrive, failure-to-gain-weight and macrocephaly concepts.
Terminology and MRCM do not diagnose delay, determine age norms or replace standardized screening.
